texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: Weekly report/ Minutes of the Crop Weather Watch Group (CWWG) meeting as on 06.04.2026
texts.text: Weekly report/ Minutes of the Crop Weather Watch Group (CWWG) meeting as on 06.04.2026

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: Key Insights
texts.text: Key Insights

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: 1. Rabi Season harvest and Summer Season Sowing Status:
texts.text: 1. Rabi Season harvest and Summer Season Sowing Status:

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig:  As on April 03, 2026, the area sown under summer crops is 58.29 lakh ha as compared to 57.80 lakh ha during the corresponding period of last year.  Of this, area of 30.12 lakh ha has been sown under Rice this year.
texts.text:  As on April 03, 2026, the area sown under summer crops is 58.29 lakh ha as compared to 57.80 lakh ha during the corresponding period of last year.  Of this, area of 30.12 lakh ha has been sown under Rice this year.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig:  As on 27.03.2026, 32.26% of area has been harvested for Rabi crops all over India. Approximately 67% area  of  Pulses  mainly  in  Gram  (66%),  Urdbean  (86%)  &  Moong  (63%);  71.56%  area  of  Oilseeds majorly in Rapeseed & Mustard (75.53%); 38.31% area of Shri Anna cum Coarse Cereals majorly in Jowar (54.47%), 25.57% area of Rice and 6.00% area of Wheat  has been harvested for Rabi crops 2025-26.
texts.text:  As on 27.03.2026, 32.26% of area has been harvested for Rabi crops all over India. Approximately 67% area  of  Pulses  mainly  in  Gram  (66%),  Urdbean  (86%)  &  Moong  (63%);  71.56%  area  of  Oilseeds majorly in Rapeseed & Mustard (75.53%); 38.31% area of Shri Anna cum Coarse Cereals majorly in Jowar (54.47%), 25.57% area of Rice and 6.00% area of Wheat  has been harvested for Rabi crops 2025-26.

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: 2. Weather Update:
texts.text: 2. Weather Update:

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Temperature: Maximum Temperature is likely to be normal to above-normal maximum temperatures (1.6-3⁰C) are likely over parts of Ladakh, along the west coast (Konkan & Goa & isolated places in coastal Karnataka), Coastal Odisha & adjoining north Coastal Andhra Pradesh & south Gangetic West Bengal,  some  parts  of  coastal  Tamil  Nadu,  some  parts  of  north  Bihar  &  adjoining  northeast  Uttar Pradesh  and  many  parts  of  northeast  India  (Assam,  Meghalaya,  Nagaland,  Manipur,  Mizoram  & Tripura).  Below  normal  to  appreciably  below  normal  maximum  temperatures  are  likely  over  central India and adjoining north peninsular India. Normal to below-normal weekly maximum temperatures are likely across the rest of the country.
texts.text: Temperature: Maximum Temperature is likely to be normal to above-normal maximum temperatures (1.6-3⁰C) are likely over parts of Ladakh, along the west coast (Konkan & Goa & isolated places in coastal Karnataka), Coastal Odisha & adjoining north Coastal Andhra Pradesh & south Gangetic West Bengal,  some  parts  of  coastal  Tamil  Nadu,  some  parts  of  north  Bihar  &  adjoining  northeast  Uttar Pradesh  and  many  parts  of  northeast  India  (Assam,  Meghalaya,  Nagaland,  Manipur,  Mizoram  & Tripura).  Below  normal  to  appreciably  below  normal  maximum  temperatures  are  likely  over  central India and adjoining north peninsular India. Normal to below-normal weekly maximum temperatures are likely across the rest of the country.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: a) Rainfall  Distribution:  From  March  01-  April  05  2026,  the  All-India  cumulative  rainfall  is  20% higher than its Long Period Average (LPA), with regional variations: (i) East & Northeast India: 52%; (ii) Northwest India: (-) 9%; (iii) Central India: 33%; and South Peninsula: 27%.
texts.text: Rainfall  Distribution:  From  March  01-  April  05  2026,  the  All-India  cumulative  rainfall  is  20% higher than its Long Period Average (LPA), with regional variations: (i) East & Northeast India: 52%; (ii) Northwest India: (-) 9%; (iii) Central India: 33%; and South Peninsula: 27%.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig:  A fresh Western Disturbance is likely to affect Northwest India from 07th April, 2026.
texts.text:  A fresh Western Disturbance is likely to affect Northwest India from 07th April, 2026.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig:  Wet spell activity with thunderstorm & lightning very likely over central, east & peninsular India till 09th April with isolated hailstorm over Chhattisgarh on 05th; West Madhya Pradesh on 08th; Sub Himalayan West Bengal & Sikkim on 05th, 07th & 08th; Bihar on 06th & 08th; Jharkhand during 06th-08th April, 2026. Isolated heavy rainfall likely over Sub-Himalayan West Bengal & Sikkim on 08th & 09th April.
texts.text:  Wet spell activity with thunderstorm & lightning very likely over central, east & peninsular India till 09th April with isolated hailstorm over Chhattisgarh on 05th; West Madhya Pradesh on 08th; Sub Himalayan West Bengal & Sikkim on 05th, 07th & 08th; Bihar on 06th & 08th; Jharkhand during 06th-08th April, 2026. Isolated heavy rainfall likely over Sub-Himalayan West Bengal & Sikkim on 08th & 09th April.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig:  Day temperatures are likely to remain below normal to near normal over most part of the country during next 7 days.
texts.text:  Day temperatures are likely to remain below normal to near normal over most part of the country during next 7 days.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig:  Widespread  rainfall  activity  likely  over  Northeast  India,  Jammu  &  Kashmir  adjoining  Punjab, Himachal Pradesh, Tamil Nadu and Isolated rainfall activity over Uttarakhand & adjoining plains of Western Himalayan region, Central India, Odisha & adjoining Coastal Andhra Pradesh.
texts.text:  Widespread  rainfall  activity  likely  over  Northeast  India,  Jammu  &  Kashmir  adjoining  Punjab, Himachal Pradesh, Tamil Nadu and Isolated rainfall activity over Uttarakhand & adjoining plains of Western Himalayan region, Central India, Odisha & adjoining Coastal Andhra Pradesh.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig:  Rainfall, Thunderstorm & Hailstorm activity over Northwest India, Central, & East India. Isolated heavy rainfall over Northeast India.
texts.text:  Rainfall, Thunderstorm & Hailstorm activity over Northwest India, Central, & East India. Isolated heavy rainfall over Northeast India.

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: 3. Inputs Situation
texts.text: 3. Inputs Situation

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: a) Water  Reservoir  Status:  As  on  02  April  2026,  the  total  live  water  storage  in  166  important reservoirs across the country (monitored by CWC) is 85.698 billion cubic meter, which is 46.69% of total live storage capacity (183.565 BCM). The current year's storage is nearly 116.44 percent of
texts.text: Water  Reservoir  Status:  As  on  02  April  2026,  the  total  live  water  storage  in  166  important reservoirs across the country (monitored by CWC) is 85.698 billion cubic meter, which is 46.69% of total live storage capacity (183.565 BCM). The current year's storage is nearly 116.44 percent of

texts.label: text
texts.orig: last  year's  storage  and  126.67  percent  of  the  average  storage  of  the  last  ten  years  during  the corresponding period. The overall storage position is better than the corresponding period of last year in the country as a whole and is also better than the Normal storage during the corresponding period.
texts.text: last  year's  storage  and  126.67  percent  of  the  average  storage  of  the  last  ten  years  during  the corresponding period. The overall storage position is better than the corresponding period of last year in the country as a whole and is also better than the Normal storage during the corresponding period.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig:  Groundwater  Levels:  In  Post-monsoon  2025,  In  January,  2026,  around  75.8%  of  the  country's monitoring stations exhibited a depth to water level within 10 meters below ground level. Deeper water levels of more than 20 m are observed in around 13.5% of stations of the country covering mainly the western states, especially Western Rajasthan, Punjab, Haryana and Chandigarh.
texts.text:  Groundwater  Levels:  In  Post-monsoon  2025,  In  January,  2026,  around  75.8%  of  the  country's monitoring stations exhibited a depth to water level within 10 meters below ground level. Deeper water levels of more than 20 m are observed in around 13.5% of stations of the country covering mainly the western states, especially Western Rajasthan, Punjab, Haryana and Chandigarh.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig:  The  deeper  groundwater  level  in  the  states  like  Western  Rajasthan,  Punjab,  Haryana  and Chandigarh  during  January,  2026  has  been  significantly  influenced  by  the  over  extraction  of groundwater compared to the recharge in the area, indicating negative water balance.
texts.text:  The  deeper  groundwater  level  in  the  states  like  Western  Rajasthan,  Punjab,  Haryana  and Chandigarh  during  January,  2026  has  been  significantly  influenced  by  the  over  extraction  of groundwater compared to the recharge in the area, indicating negative water balance.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig:  About 70% of the analyzed stations of the unconfined aquifer experienced a rise of water level in January, 2026 when compared to the decadal mean water level of 2016-2025.
texts.text:  About 70% of the analyzed stations of the unconfined aquifer experienced a rise of water level in January, 2026 when compared to the decadal mean water level of 2016-2025.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig:  The  Groundwater  Drought  Indices  indicate  that  in  January,  2026,  groundwater  conditions  across India remained largely stable, with 90.30% of monitoring stations falling under normal condition in respect of Ground Water Drought. However, localized deficits were observed in several regions notably in Arunachal Pradesh, Himachal Pradesh, Meghalaya, Nagaland.
texts.text:  The  Groundwater  Drought  Indices  indicate  that  in  January,  2026,  groundwater  conditions  across India remained largely stable, with 90.30% of monitoring stations falling under normal condition in respect of Ground Water Drought. However, localized deficits were observed in several regions notably in Arunachal Pradesh, Himachal Pradesh, Meghalaya, Nagaland.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: b) Fertilizers: The fertilizer supply remains stable across all products.
texts.text: Fertilizers: The fertilizer supply remains stable across all products.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: 4. Pests & Diseases : The overall intensity of pests and diseases remained below the Economic Threshold Level.
texts.text: Pests & Diseases : The overall intensity of pests and diseases remained below the Economic Threshold Level.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: 5. Crops whose Mandi Wholesale prices are below MSP:
texts.text: Crops whose Mandi Wholesale prices are below MSP:

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: 1. RAINFALL
texts.text: 1. RAINFALL

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig:  During the week (26 March - 01 April 2026), higher rainfall was observed, as compared to Long Period Average (LPA) by 25%. Cumulative seasonal rainfall (01 st March to 01 April 2026) is higher than LPA by 14%.
texts.text:  During the week (26 March - 01 April 2026), higher rainfall was observed, as compared to Long Period Average (LPA) by 25%. Cumulative seasonal rainfall (01 st March to 01 April 2026) is higher than LPA by 14%.

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: Rainfall Forecast:
texts.text: Rainfall Forecast:

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: · Overall, thunderstorm activity is likely to continue over the northeast & adjoining east India during the week.
texts.text: Overall, thunderstorm activity is likely to continue over the northeast & adjoining east India during the week.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: · Overall, rainfall is likely to be near normal to above normal over parts of northwest India and near normal over northeast India; it is likely to be below normal over the remaining parts of the country.
texts.text: Overall, rainfall is likely to be near normal to above normal over parts of northwest India and near normal over northeast India; it is likely to be below normal over the remaining parts of the country.

texts.text: 2. TEMPERATURE: MIN & MAX

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: Week 1 (03 April - 09 April 2026):
texts.text: Week 1 (03 April - 09 April 2026):

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig:  Maximum Temperature is likely to be above normal in North East India, many parts of Bihar, many parts of J&K and in coastal regions of Odisha, Coastal Andhra Pradesh and Kerala.
texts.text:  Maximum Temperature is likely to be above normal in North East India, many parts of Bihar, many parts of J&K and in coastal regions of Odisha, Coastal Andhra Pradesh and Kerala.

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: Week 2 (10 April - 16 April 2026):
texts.text: Week 2 (10 April - 16 April 2026):

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig:  Low Probability  of  Heat  Wave  conditions  likely  during  the  second  half  of  the  week  over  Coastal Odisha and adjoining north Coastal Andhra Pradesh.
texts.text:  Low Probability  of  Heat  Wave  conditions  likely  during  the  second  half  of  the  week  over  Coastal Odisha and adjoining north Coastal Andhra Pradesh.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig:  Appreciably  above  normal  (+3.1  to  +5.0°C)  maximum  temperatures  are  likely  over  some  parts  of interior & coastal Odisha and adjoining Coastal Andhra Pradesh; normal to above-normal maximum temperatures ((+1.6 to +3.0°C) are likely over some parts of the East, South and Western parts of India (Gangetic West Bengal, Odisha, Coastal Andhra Pradesh, Tamil Nadu, southern parts of Kerala, coastal  regions  of  Gujarat  state,  Konkan,  Goa  and  coastal  Karnataka).  Normal  to  below  normal weekly maximum temperatures are likely across the rest of the country.
texts.text:  Appreciably  above  normal  (+3.1  to  +5.0°C)  maximum  temperatures  are  likely  over  some  parts  of interior & coastal Odisha and adjoining Coastal Andhra Pradesh; normal to above-normal maximum temperatures ((+1.6 to +3.0°C) are likely over some parts of the East, South and Western parts of India (Gangetic West Bengal, Odisha, Coastal Andhra Pradesh, Tamil Nadu, southern parts of Kerala, coastal  regions  of  Gujarat  state,  Konkan,  Goa  and  coastal  Karnataka).  Normal  to  below  normal weekly maximum temperatures are likely across the rest of the country.

texts.orig: Rabi Crops - Area coverage
texts.text: Rabi Crops - Area coverage

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig:  Rice : About 30.12 lakh ha area coverage under Summer Rice has been reported as compared to 32.59 lakh ha during the corresponding period of last year.
texts.text:  Rice : About 30.12 lakh ha area coverage under Summer Rice has been reported as compared to 32.59 lakh ha during the corresponding period of last year.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig:  Pulses :  About  8.79  lakh  ha  area  coverage  has  been  reported  compared  to  7.02  lakh  ha  during  the corresponding period of last year.
texts.text:  Pulses :  About  8.79  lakh  ha  area  coverage  has  been  reported  compared  to  7.02  lakh  ha  during  the corresponding period of last year.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig:  Oilseeds :  About 7.74 lakh ha area coverage has been reported compared to 7.42 lakh ha during the corresponding period of last year.
texts.text:  Oilseeds :  About 7.74 lakh ha area coverage has been reported compared to 7.42 lakh ha during the corresponding period of last year.

texts.text: * Normal area from 2022-23 to 2024-25.

texts.text: As on 02 April, 2026, the current live storage in 166 reservoirs was 85.698 BCM (46.69%) of total live capacity of 183.565 BCM.

table_cells.text: SOWING AND HARVESTING STATUS OF RABI CROPS AS ON 03.04.2026
Harvesting is going on in the states like Karnataka (98%), Gujarat (90%), Maharashtra (17%), MP (40%), Rajasthan (50%) & in remaining states, harvesting will be started in 1st or 2nd week of April.
Tamil Nadu (85%), Kerala (80%), Tripura (50%), Andhra Pradesh (20%), etc has already been harvested.
Overall around 82% area has been harvested mainly in Gram (82%), Lentil (66%), Fieldpae (94%), Urdbean (93%), Moongbean (69%), etc has already been harvested.
100% harvested in Gujarat & Andhra Pradesh. Maharashtra (95%), Karnataka (85%), Madhya Pradesh (70%), Rajasthan (70%), Chhattisgarh (85%), UP (60%), etc has already been harvested.

Harvesting is going on in a full swing.
Overall around 44% area has been harvested mainly in Jowar (59%), Maize (36%), Barley (20%), etc has already been harvested.

UP (12%), Maharashtra (15%), Rajasthan (25%), MP (13%), etc has already been harvested.
Overall around 94% area has been harvested mainly in Rapeseed & Mustard (99%), Groundnut (38%), Safflower (83%), Sunflower (78%), Linseed (86%), etc has already been harvested.
100% harvested in Assam, Bihar, Gujarat, Haryana, Jharkhand, MP, Punjab, Rajasthan, West Bengal, Karnataka, & Telangana. UP (90%), AP (62%), Chhattisgarh (88%), etc has already been harvested.
